_metadata.for_match: Austin FC vs Houston Dynamo FC
_metadata.match_time: TBD — confirm kickoff time before pipeline runs (Copa Tejas / MLS Matchday 9)
_metadata.competition: 2026 MLS Regular Season — Matchday 9 | Copa Tejas Round 1
_metadata.notes: Austin FC away guide. COPA TEJAS ROUND 1. Austin: 13th West, 1W-3D-3L, 26th in MLS xG (4.1). Main DP: Facundo Torres (Uruguayan, from Palmeiras, former Orlando City). Louisville beat Austin 2-1 in Open Cup R32 (April 14). Houston hosts Louisville in Open Cup R16 on April 29. Research cache expired Apr 24 — run pipeline with --fresh flag (full Sonar run ~60 min). SD result: 1-0 W, Aliyu 35' (first goal of season), second consecutive clean sheet, THIRD straight win. Houston record: 4W-0D-4L, 12 pts after 8 MLS matches. Guilherme stats: check if he scored vs SD (Aliyu was the confirmed scorer). HANDLES CONFIRMED: Herrera @h.herrera16 (1.1M, VERIFIED April 21). Guilherme @guilherme11 (VERIFIED). Facundo Torres @facundotorres10 still UNVERIFIED — needs PC scan before pipeline. Bogusz baby angle is STALE (in 3+ guides) — rotate out or reframe with fresh post if available. Kickoff time for April 25: TBD — confirm before pipeline.

matchday_hook: Copa Tejas Round 1 is HERE. Houston heads to Q2 Stadium on Saturday to open the Texas trophy series — and after back-to-back 1-0 wins (Orlando on Herrera's birthday 🎂, San Diego at home on Wednesday), the Dynamo are on a THREE-MATCH winning streak. Two clean sheets in a row. Ibrahim Aliyu just scored his first goal of the season. This team is clicking. Verde vs Orange. Trophy match. Road warriors. The vibe is real right now. 🧡
vibe_strip_items: 🏆 COPA TEJAS ROUND 1 — Houston and Austin fight for the Texas trophy. Verde vs Orange. It matters.
✅ San Diego result: 1-0 WIN. Aliyu 35' — first goal of the season for the Nigerian forward. Second consecutive clean sheet. THREE straight wins.
✅ Orlando result before that: 1-0 WIN. Herrera 75' on his 36th birthday. STARTED and scored.
📈 Houston record: 4W-0D-4L through 8 MLS matches. 12 points. Momentum is real.
🇳🇬 Ibrahim Aliyu — the man of the moment. Nigerian forward, 24, Kano native. Former Columbus Crew. Came back to Houston in March and just delivered his first goal of the season at the perfect time.
📈 Guilherme: 9 goal contributions (5G/4A) through 7 MLS matches. First Dynamo player to reach 9 in his first 6 MLS matches since Brian Ching in 2006 — TWENTY years.
🇺🇾 Facundo Torres (Austin DP) — 0 goals in 2026 but 3 assists. Is THIS the Copa Tejas match he finally converts? He is genuinely dangerous.
🚗 Road warriors on a THREE-MATCH win streak. Q2 Stadium has never felt so accessible.
⚽ Austin FC: 13th Western Conference, 1W-3D-3L. 26th in MLS for expected goals. The Lions are vulnerable.
🏆 April 29: Louisville City FC at Shell Energy in Open Cup R16 — Houston is two weeks away from advancing to the quarterfinal.
💪 Artur: fully back and available. McGlynn status TBD for April 25 — check official matchday report.

hot_posts.description: Nigerian forward, 24, from Kano. Scored his FIRST goal of the 2026 season in the 35th minute vs San Diego FC — the winner in a 1-0 home victory. This is his 'arrival' moment after returning to Houston from Columbus Crew in March. Look for any post-match celebration or reaction content.
hot_posts.why_it_works: First goal of the season + homecoming narrative + team win. Timing is perfect.
hot_posts.content_angle: He came back. And now he delivered. The Nigerian forward who returned home and scored when it mattered. This is the discovery framing for Austin — someone new who just proved they belong.

hot_posts.post_date: April 2026 — check for Orlando road trip content before pipeline runs
hot_posts.description: Bicycle kick in 78 seconds (Open Cup vs El Paso) — the moment that started this run. MLS Energy Moment of the Matchday.
hot_posts.why_it_works: Viral highlight. Player awareness + shareable moment. Reposts his own goals.
hot_posts.content_angle: Lead with the 78-second moment every time until something tops it.

hot_posts.post_date: April 2026 — check for post-Orlando road trip content before pipeline runs
hot_posts.description: Brazilian winger (listed as 'Augusto' on lineup cards — same person). 9 goal contributions (5G/4A) through 7 MLS matches. Pace through first 6 MLS matches broke a 20-year club record — first Dynamo player to 9 contributions in opening 6 matches since Brian Ching in 2006. Still tied for most in all of MLS. Turned 31 on April 13. Neymar publicly DMd him.
hot_posts.why_it_works: Historic pace is a shareable stat. '20-year record' is the kind of number that travels.
hot_posts.content_angle: Lead with the 20-year record. Then the birthday. Then Neymar. Individual brilliance — frame as star power, not team creator.

hot_posts.description: Baby girl due July 2026. Every road game is a countdown to being home for the birth.
hot_posts.why_it_works: Most universally relatable content angle. New parents love this.
hot_posts.content_angle: Underappreciated workhorse + new dad countdown. He works hard, Guilherme gets the glory.

hot_posts.description: Started and scored the match-winner in Orlando on his 36th birthday. Club legend returned to Houston for 2026. Mexican international, former Porto, former Atlético Madrid. Artur says they're great friends and 'he still plays at a high level.' Birthday goal in a road win — this is a gift-wrapped content moment.
hot_posts.why_it_works: 36th birthday goal on the road. Legend returns. Universal 'never stop' story.
hot_posts.content_angle: 36 and STILL doing it. Not a sub-on cameo — he STARTED and won the game. This is the main character energy for the Austin guide.

hot_posts.description: Uruguayan DP winger. Former Orlando City leading scorer. Signed Jan 2026 from Palmeiras ($9.5M). Uruguay national team. 0G/3A in 2026 — creating but not converting.
hot_posts.why_it_works: The name to know as a threat. South American flair angle plays well.
hot_posts.content_angle: Enemy respect framing. He is genuinely dangerous and the audience should know who he is.

hot_posts.description: Albanian international forward. Scored multiple times this season including vs LA Galaxy. Second goal of season confirmed.
hot_posts.why_it_works: Discovery framing — Albanian international is rare in MLS. Audience will be curious.
hot_posts.content_angle: The X-factor most people have not heard of. Who IS this guy and why is he a threat?

hot_posts.description: American CB from Denver. Baby daughter. Into golf, community work, intellectual development. Signed extension through Dec 2026. Scored opening header vs Minnesota (Torres assist).
hot_posts.why_it_works: Family content + American story. Passes the wholesome test.
hot_posts.content_angle: Defensive anchor who is also a community guy. Respectful opponent, not a villain.

injury_flags.impact: Defensive depth affected. CB pairing adjustment.

injury_flags.status: AVAILABLE — returned as 2H sub vs Orlando, on bench vs San Diego. Fit and available.
injury_flags.impact: Depth CDM presence. Check if Olsen starts him vs Austin.

injury_flags.status: OUT — lower body, missed San Diego match. Status for April 25 TBD — check official report.
injury_flags.impact: MF depth. Samassékou + Bouzat pivot again if he's out.

injury_flags.status: OUT — hip injury, added to injury report April 22.
injury_flags.impact: Rookie depth defender. Low impact on starting XI.

copa_tejas_context.format: Round-robin among Texas MLS clubs. Trophy awarded at end of season.
copa_tejas_context.hook: This is not just a regular season match. The Copa Tejas is a real trophy with real bragging rights. Houston vs Austin in Austin. Verde vs Orange. The Texas rivalry with stakes attached.
